hero.title: A fully autonomous AI agent in 678 KB
hero.sub: One static binary that connects 50+ model providers (OpenAI, Anthropic, Ollama and more) to Telegram, Discord, Slack, Signal and 15 more chat channels — memory, tool use, cron scheduling and sandboxing built in. No Node, no Python, nothing but libc: it runs in about 1 MB of RAM, small enough for a $5 ARM board.

alts.label: Build from source (Zig 0.16.0)
alts.code:
git clone https://github.com/nullclaw/nullclaw.git
cd nullclaw
zig build -Doptimize=ReleaseSmall

quickstart:
Onboard once, then talk to it.

```bash
# quick setup with a provider key
nullclaw onboard --api-key sk-... --provider openrouter

# send a single message
nullclaw agent -m "Hello, nullclaw!"

# start the long-running runtime on 127.0.0.1:3000
nullclaw gateway

# health checks
nullclaw status && nullclaw doctor
```

features.title: 50+ providers, one config
features.body: OpenRouter, Anthropic, OpenAI, Gemini, Ollama, Groq, Bedrock and more — plus any OpenAI-compatible endpoint via a custom base URL.

features.title: 19 messaging channels
features.body: Telegram, Discord, Signal, Slack, WhatsApp, Matrix, IRC, Email, Nostr and others. Each channel sits behind its own allowlist; empty means deny.

features.title: Memory with hybrid search
features.body: The agent remembers past conversations and facts in SQLite, and finds them again with keyword search (FTS5) and embedding similarity combined. Nine other backends — from plain Markdown files to PostgreSQL and Redis — when you need them.

features.title: Sandboxed by default
features.body: Commands the agent runs are isolated in a sandbox: NullClaw auto-picks the best backend available on your system (Landlock, Firejail, Bubblewrap or Docker). File access is limited to the agent's workspace, and API keys are encrypted at rest with ChaCha20-Poly1305.

features.title: Speaks A2A, MCP and ACP
features.body: Other agents can discover and call it over Google's A2A protocol (v0.3.0, JSON-RPC). It can use tools from any MCP (Model Context Protocol) server over stdio or HTTP, and an adapter lets code editors that speak the Agent Client Protocol (ACP) drive it directly.

features.title: OpenClaw-compatible
features.body: Uses the same config format as OpenClaw, the TypeScript AI assistant — same structure, same snake_case keys. `nullclaw migrate openclaw` imports your existing config and memory in one command.

note: Pre-1.0: config and CLI may change between releases. Size and memory figures are measured by the project.

plain.what: NullClaw is an AI assistant runtime: a single 678 KB static binary, written in Zig, that runs an LLM-backed agent you can message from Telegram, Discord, Slack, Signal and 15 other channels, backed by any of 50+ model providers.
plain.why: Comparable agent stacks run on Node.js or Python and need 100 MB to over 1 GB of RAM; NullClaw compiles to one small native binary that does the same job in about 1 MB, so an always-on assistant fits on a $5 ARM board or in a corner of any server.
plain.when: Reach for it when you want your own always-on AI bot — say, a Telegram or Discord assistant with persistent memory — running 24/7 on a Raspberry Pi, a VPS or an edge device, without maintaining a heavy runtime.
